[[File:Elder maul detail.png|left|170px]]
The '''elder maul''' is a two-handed [[crush weapon]] that requires [[Attack]] and [[Strength]] levels of 75 to wield. It can be obtained as a possible reward from the [[Chambers of Xeric]].

It is the highest tier crush weapon available in ''[[Old School RuneScape]]''. It overtakes the [[godsword]]s as the weapon with the highest [[strength bonus]]. Despite the strength bonus, the [[abyssal bludgeon]] often has higher overall damage output against low-defence monsters because of its quicker [[attack speed]], making it only useful in places such as [[Tekton]]. Because of its high strength bonus, it is often used as a finisher in [[player killing]].

Although its normal attack has fairly limited use cases, its special attack surpasses that of the [[dragon warhammer]] and is best in slot for many bossing encounters and raids that utilise defence reduction including the [[Corporeal Beast]], the [[Chambers of Xeric]], and the [[Theatre of Blood]].

As with the [[dragon warhammer]], it too can be used as a substitute for a regular [[hammer]] when banging the big door at the entrance of [[Bandos' Stronghold]] in the [[God Wars Dungeon]], as well as in the [[Chambers of Xeric]] to stun [[Jewelled Crab]]s and build any [[Small storage unit|storage chest]] (with the required [[mallignum root plank]]s and [[Construction]] level).

[[File:Elder maul attack.gif|thumb|240px|A player attacks an opponent with the elder maul.]]

==Special attack==
The elder maul has a [[special attack]], ''Pulverize'', costing 50% special attack energy. Increasing the player's accuracy by 25%, a [[successful hit]] will reduce the target's '''current''' Defence level by 35%, effectively acting as a superior version of the [[dragon warhammer]]'s special attack. Even then, due to the maul's stats, it is slightly more accurate than a dragon warhammer with an [[avernic defender]], beating the pair by +12 crush bonus and +54 strength bonus.

Depending on the player's weapons, the maul can be an upgrade or downgrade in terms of inventory spots. For example, if a player is using another two-handed weapon alongside the maul, their inventory space will not be affected. However, if the player is using a one-handed weapon and a shield/defender with a full inventory, one item must be dropped in order to equip the maul.

When used against [[Tekton]], the first special attack from an elder maul is guaranteed to hit, however, if subsequent attacks were to fail to hit, it will instead reduce 5% of his Defence level.

Defence level reductions '''will''' stack over multiple successful hits; each reduction is calculated from the current temporary level (so follow-up drains reduce fewer levels). The Defence level of the target after each respective hit, with no rounding, would be 65%, 42.25%, 27.5%, 17.85%, 11.6% then 7.5% of the original. However, fractional defence levels reduced by the special attack will be rounded down.

For example, on a monster with 75 Defence, the first special attack will reduce 26 levels (rounded down from 26.25), leaving the enemy at 49 Defence. The next special attack will then reduce 17 levels (rounded down from 17.15), leaving the enemy at 32 Defence.

'''Note:''' The special attack animation has a 1-tick delay over a regular attack, meaning if you are [[flinching]] a monster like [[Kalphite Queen]] you will be attacked.

==Trivia==
* The elder maul was available from the supplies table in an [[unrestricted world]] during the week of 18 August - [[25 August]] [[2016]] as a [[Elder maul (beta)|test weapon]] for players. As it was used solely for testing its power, a magenta [[Tzhaar-ket-om]] was used as a graphics placeholder.
